_README: Dataset B (functions half). The sector-agnostic decision seats a B2B persona can occupy. AUTHORED from OFO role families, not downloaded — no survey ships 'decision criteria'. Each record is a FROZEN mandate the texture LLM may voice but never rewrite. budget_authority_band is a DECLARED field of (function x seniority), never an LLM number and never a consumer income line — this is what keeps the can-the-org-approve question assertable with the model off. REVIEW each record against how the seat actually behaves in your target customers' orgs.
_provenance: ofo_2023_role_families (DHET OFO register) + standard B2B buying-committee roles

procurement.label: Procurement / Buyer
procurement.ofo_family: Supply, distribution and procurement managers (1324) / Buyers (3323)
procurement.mandate: Sources and contracts goods/services for the organisation. Owns the commercial terms of the deal, not the use of the product. Gatekeeps spend.
decision_criteria: unit cost / total cost of ownership
does it fit an approved budget line
typical_objections: price isn't competitive vs incumbents
you have no reference customers our size
terms are too rigid / lock us in
you're not on our approved-vendor list
procurement.budget_authority_band: approves within a delegated spend limit; above it escalates to finance/exco
procurement.what_wins_them: lower TCO, proven references, flexible terms, de-risked onboarding
procurement.dmu_mandate: In the buying-committee (DMU) model this seat controls vendor ACCESS and the commercial transaction. Can advance or kill a deal procedurally (vendor onboarding, RFP rules) independent of whether the org wants the product. Rarely the sole yes, often the decisive no.
procurement.consensus_behaviour: Will not champion; defends process. Stalls deals that skip procurement or lack a clean paper trail. Aligns once the line-owner and finance both signal yes AND terms are clean.
stalls_deal_when: vendor bypassed procurement to reach the line-owner
no competitive benchmark / sole-source can't be justified
procurement.decision_provenance: ofo_2023 + DMU/buying-committee model (Webster & Wind; Gartner buying-group research)

line_owner.label: Line / Operational owner
line_owner.mandate: Runs the team or process the product would touch. Accountable for an operational KPI. Judges the deal by impact on THEIR numbers and workflow, not company-wide strategy.
decision_criteria: integration & switching cost to my team
typical_objections: my team has no bandwidth to adopt this now
it doesn't fit how we actually work
the switching cost outweighs the upside
what happens when it breaks mid-shift
line_owner.budget_authority_band: controls a departmental budget line; larger commitments need finance sign-off
line_owner.what_wins_them: clear KPI lift, low-friction integration, strong support, phased rollout

finance.label: Finance / CFO
finance.mandate: Guards the balance sheet. Approves or blocks spend on ROI and cash terms. Cares about the business case, not the product features.
typical_objections: the payback is too slow
this is opex we didn't budget
the business case is unproven
we're cutting discretionary spend this year
finance.budget_authority_band: holds high sign-off authority; largest commitments go to board/exco
finance.what_wins_them: hard ROI numbers, short payback, low downside, fits the budget cycle

partnerships.label: Partnerships / Business Development
partnerships.ofo_family: Sales, marketing and business development managers (1221)
partnerships.mandate: Builds external relationships and deals on behalf of the org. The seat that says yes to a PARTNERSHIP (vs a purchase). Weighs strategic fit and brand, often over near-term cost.
decision_criteria: brand and reputational risk of associating
typical_objections: this isn't core to our strategy
the value split doesn't work for us
we can't be seen partnering with an unproven brand
we'd want exclusivity you can't give
partnerships.budget_authority_band: commits partnership resources within a mandate; major deals need exco/board
partnerships.what_wins_them: clear strategic fit, attractive value share, low brand risk, a fast pilot path

compliance_csr.label: Compliance / Social & Ethics / CSR
compliance_csr.ofo_family: Policy and planning / regulatory affairs managers; sustainability officers
compliance_csr.mandate: Ensures the org meets its regulatory, governance and social-responsibility obligations. Judges a proposal by whether it helps DISCHARGE a mandate (compliance credit, social impact, audit) rather than by commercial return.
decision_criteria: regulatory / charter credit it earns us
typical_objections: does this actually count toward our obligations
we can't evidence this to an auditor
the reputational risk outweighs the credit
it doesn't align with what we've committed publicly
compliance_csr.budget_authority_band: directs a compliance/SED budget tied to obligations; spend justified by mandate, not ROI
compliance_csr.what_wins_them: demonstrable compliance/charter credit, clean audit trail, real measurable impact

champion.label: End-user / Internal champion
champion.ofo_family: Professional/specialist who would USE the product (role varies)
champion.mandate: The person who would actually USE the thing day-to-day and, if convinced, lobbies internally for it. Holds no budget but can make or kill a deal through advocacy or silence. Reacts on personal/professional benefit, then on whether they can sell it upward.
decision_criteria: does it make MY job better / easier
is it credible to me as a practitioner
can I sell this to my boss
typical_objections: this won't survive contact with how I really work
I can't get my manager to fund it
I've seen tools like this overpromise
it's more work before it's less work
champion.budget_authority_band: no budget authority; influences through advocacy and adoption
champion.what_wins_them: obvious personal benefit, practitioner credibility, easy to justify upward, low day-one friction
